spoken: Your AI agent just looked at Amazon. Here's what it saw.
A hundred and forty-two interactive elements on one Amazon product page. The sieve found every button, every dropdown, every price tag. No LLM. No vision model. Zero tokens.
Classify the whole page in seconds. Clickable. Readable. Typable. Done.

The agent connects through our MCP server. It sees the glossary. Amazon dot add-to-cart is clickable. Amazon dot price is readable. Amazon dot quantity-selector is selectable. Structured. Deterministic.
Without LeftGlove? The agent takes a screenshot. Sends it to a vision model. Burns two thousand tokens. And it still guesses wrong on the selector.
With LeftGlove? Zero tokens. The agent knows exactly what's on the page. Every element named, located, typed.
Taming the most complex DOM on the web.
Now let's try something harder. A state park reservation form. Date pickers. Dropdowns. Availability grids. Everyone's favorite.
Eighty-seven elements. Every form field mapped. Every dropdown option inventoried. Every date picker decoded.
The glossary gives the agent a vocabulary for the form. Park selector. Arrival date. Departure date. Campsite type. Search button. All named. All typed.
Book site forty-seven at Big Basin for the Fourth of July weekend. Six steps.
Select the park. Fill the dates. Pick tent camping. Search availability. Reserve the site. Every action uses a vocabulary name. Not a CSS selector. Not a guess.
Six steps. Zero fumbling. Your AI just booked your campsite.
The sieve sees everything. The glossary names everything. The agent knows everything. LeftGlove plus OpenClaw. Deterministic page understanding for AI agents.
